author: Athanasius of Alexandria
book: On the Incarnation of the Word.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: On the Incarnation of the Word.
Prophecies of the Cross. How these prophecies are satisfied in Christ alone.

title: Prophecies of the Cross. How these prophecies are satisfied in Christ alone.

source.title: NPNF2-04. Athanasius: Select Works and Letters

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Prophecies of the Cross.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: How these prophecies are satisfied in Christ alone.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But, perhaps, having heard the prophecy of His death, you ask to learn also what is set forth concerning the Cross.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For not even this is passed over: it is displayed by the holy men with great plainness.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For first Moses predicts it, and that with a loud voice, when he says: “Ye shall see your Life hanging before your eyes, and shall not believe.”

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: And next, the prophets after him witness of this, saying: “But I as an innocent lamb brought to be slain, knew it not; they counselled an evil counsel against me, saying, Hither and let us cast a tree upon his bread, and efface him from the land of the living.”

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: And again: “They pierced my hands and my feet, they numbered all my bones, they parted my garments among them, and for my vesture they cast lots.”

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Now a death raised aloft and that takes place on a tree, could be none other than the Cross: and again, in no other death are the hands and feet pierced, save on the Cross only.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But since by the sojourn of the Saviour among men all nations also on every side began to know God; they did not leave this point, either, without a reference: but mention is made of this matter as well in the Holy Scriptures.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For “there shall be,” he saith, “the root of Jesse, and he that riseth to rule the nations, on him shall the nations hope.”

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: This then is a little in proof of what has happened.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But all Scripture teems with refutations of the disbelief of the Jews.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For which of the righteous men and holy prophets, and patriarchs, recorded in the divine Scriptures, ever had his corporal birth of a virgin only?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Or what woman has sufficed without man for the conception of human kind?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Was not Abel born of Adam, Enoch of Jared, Noe of Lamech, and Abraham of Tharra, Isaac of Abraham, Jacob of Isaac?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Was not Judas born of Jacob, and Moses and Aaron of Ameram?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Was not Samuel born of Elkana, was not David of Jesse, was not Solomon of David, was not Ezechias of Achaz, was not Josias of Amos, was not Esaias of Amos, was not Jeremy of Chelchias, was not Ezechiel of Buzi?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Had not each a father as author of his existence?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Who then is he that is born of a virgin only?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For the prophet made exceeding much of this sign.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Or whose birth did a star in the skies forerun, to announce to the world him that was born?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: For when Moses was born, he was hid by his parents: David was not heard of, even by those of his neighbourhood, inasmuch as even the great Samuel knew him not, but asked, had Jesse yet another son?

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: Abraham again became known to his neighbours as a great man only subsequently to his birth.

verses.book: On the Incarnation of the Word.
verses.text: But of Christ’s birth the witness was not man, but a star in that heaven whence He was descending.
